bug: The TS2559/TS2560 weak-type diagnostic ('has no properties in common with type') rendered the source as the un-widened fresh object literal ('{ c: 1 }') instead of its widened apparent type ('{ c: number }'), diverging from tsc. Fix routes the TS2559 source slot through the shared widen_fresh_object_literal_properties_for_display helper.

For a failing union source (e.g. 'number | undefined' -> 'string'), tsz elaborated the wrong constituent ('number') because it iterated stored printer order (nullish last) instead of tsc's relation order (nullish first). Fix adds reorder_union_members_nullish_first and iterates it at the elaboration site only.

A type alias whose body is a template-literal (`${"a"|"b"}-x`) or string-mapping intrinsic (Capitalize<...>) reducing to a finite literal union was printed by its ALIAS NAME instead of the expanded union that tsc shows. Fix marks such bodies 'computed' in alias_declaration_body_is_computed so is_computed_body skips the alias name.

Under exactOptionalPropertyTypes, TS2375 on the variable-assignment and array-element paths dropped the nested relation-reason elaboration ('Types of property X are incompatible. / Type undefined is not assignable to type number.') because it used DiagnosticRenderRequest::simple. Fix routes those paths through analyze_assignability_failure like the sibling TS2379 argument path.

search_global_candidates (error_reporter/suggestions.rs) gated the intrinsic keyword candidates on `meaning & TYPE`, which is nonzero even in pure value position because VALUE and TYPE flag masks overlap (CLASS/ENUM/ENUM_MEMBER). This leaked lowercase type keywords into value position (numbr->number instead of the value constructor Number) and never offered the global value symbol `undefined` (udefined gave bare TS2304). Fix gates each keyword set on meaning-exclusive bits.

A function-typed-property member whose call signatures differ only in return type (compute: () => string vs () => number) rendered the generic `Types of property 'compute' are incompatible.` header plus a `() => S`/`() => T` function-type line, which no tsc version emits. tsc collapses BOTH method and property syntax into a single TS2201 frame `The types returned by 'compute()' are incompatible...`. Fix routes the ReturnTypeMismatch reason uniformly through render_member_return_type_mismatch (suffix `()` for zero-arg, `(...)` otherwise) and removes the dead is_method branch/helpers.

tsz blanket-cleared every all-symbol missing-property list, collapsing object relation failures on late-bound-symbol members (e.g. [Symbol.dispose]) to a flat TypeMismatch with no member listed and no elaboration. A failed sync `using` (TS2850) thus lost tsc's nested "Property '[Symbol.dispose]' is missing in type 'X' but required in type 'Disposable'." tail. Fix: keep symbol missing members for non-array targets, narrow the MissingProperty guard to __js_ctor_brand_*, and route sync using through the assignability gateway.

condition_antecedent_requires_defer in the backward flow walk (flow/control_flow/core/flow_traversal.rs) recognized CALL/ASSIGNMENT/LABEL/ARRAY_MUTATION antecedents as deferrable but not AWAIT_POINT/YIELD_POINT, so an await/yield suspension between a narrowing assignment and a following `if` re-widened the reference to its declared type, emitting a false TS18048 (and a cascading TS2322 on the inferred Promise<T|undefined> return).

Under --noLib, register_function_def_ids_early (and boxed/array base registration) bailed on !has_lib_loaded(), so a user-declared global `interface Array<T>` was never wired as the array base type. A union of array-likes (number[]|string[]).every(...) then reported a false TS2339 plus a cascading TS7006 on the callback. Fix adds `&& !self.has_user_declared_registerable_global()` to the early-return gate.
